# Analysis of the efficacy of pre-lecture video and livestreaming in COMP 110.

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="<custom-path>/static/imgs/logo.png" alt="Image of Comp110 rainbow logo. "  width="500"/>

## Data and analysisPre-Lecture Videos:
 


Pre-Lecture Videos:
  3: 5.8%
  7: 33.5%
  4: 14.0%
  1: 4.1%
  2: 3.4%
  5: 22.3%
  6: 16.9%

Additional Livestreams:
  5: 19.7%
  6: 16.3%
  4: 14.0%
  2: 3.7%
  3: 6.9%
  7: 37.6%
  1: 1.7%

  It would appear as if student support for pre-lecture videos and livestreams is strong. For pre-lecture videos, 72.7% of students gave a score of 5 or more in terms of desire. That means that almost three quarters of the students feel that these videos would be have a positive impact on their learning experience. For livestreams, 73.6% of students gave a score of 5 or more. Based on this evidence, it seems that both livestreams and pre-lecture videos would be very beneficial for the learning experience of the students. If it could be implemented without too great of a cost, I think that it would be well worth the COMP department's time to look into this.
